content.claim: Hungary displays the highest student segregation by socioeconomic status in the EU (PISA 2022), with 71% of between-school reading-performance variance (vs 36% OECD average), and early selective tracks are identified by independent researchers as one structural amplifier of this segregation alongside free primary-school choice
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Segregation is multiply determined; 6/8-year tracks are one amplifier, not sole cause
Causality between track structure and national inequality not experimentally tested in Hungarian system
Cross-sectional correlations do not prove that removing tracks would reduce segregation proportionally
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
